What's happening? Alright, in this video I'm going to show you how to build your retargeting audiences for Facebook ads. People when iOS happened, everyone got all freaked out thinking like, oh my gosh, we're going to lose our ability to retarget people, but in reality it's all totally fine. So I'm going to show you how to build your retargeting list in Facebook. We're going to click a lot of buttons in this video. It's moderately tedious. This is the kind of thing you could watch while you have Netflix on TV and sort of just kind of do it along with me and enjoy the process. Nothing particularly difficult, but it is a little bit tedious. But the good news is you only have to do it one time, generally speaking. So once you have it built, it's just growing forever. Alright, so you'll often hear me call this the invisible list and it's called the invisible list because nobody can see how many people are on there except you. This is really different from how many followers you have on social or any of that kind of stuff. None of that stuff really matters, by the way. It's all about people who have actually engaged with your ads. So someone clicks on your ad, opens up your ad, reads the copy, makes a comment or whatever. They end up being in your retargeting audience or also on your invisible list. It's crazy. And in my opinion, this is almost as valuable as an email list because these are your warm aspires. They're easier to indoctrinate, by the way. So if you want to start really scaling up your brand and showing content to people on social media, these are the people that you want to show it to. The ones who have already engaged with your stuff. These are the people who are most likely to buy your stuff because they've already clicked on ads. Just from the campaigns that we've started in this class, if you were following along with me and was to say you did exactly what I did, you're already building a retargeting audience, which is great. And one of the things about retargeting audiences is it's not just made up of video viewers. So one strategy, which is kind of okay, is you run a bunch of video ads and then people watch them and you retarget the ones who watch them. This isn't really what I'm advising you to do. There's all kinds of ways that people can get on your invisible list. Again, this has nothing to do with iOS 14 or 15 or 2000 or whatever. So obviously someone watches a video ad on Facebook, like the one that we made earlier during our horizontal scaling. That would cause them to join. And what's really cool is you'll see you can segment it based on how long they watch, which you want to do. If they click on your ad, if they click the link in your ad, two totally different things, by the way. If they visit any web page with your pixel on it, that's the only thing that's kind of affected by iOS. But not really. It's not really that big of a deal, especially because if they click the link in your Facebook ad, Facebook tracks what they did on the platform. It doesn't matter what they did on your page. So you still get tracked. If they comment, if they react, like put a little heart emoji, or if they forward your post or ad, or if they visit your Facebook or Instagram page, this is getting forever. So I'm just going to show you how to do this. So I'm going to do it for you on video. And again, if you'll just watch this and do it with me, just put something on Netflix or listen to some music or whatever, this isn't hard. It's just kind of tedious. And I'm going to go through it pretty quickly. And I've also included screenshots in this PDF for you. So you can watch it, or not watch it, but you can go through these if you want to, and just see how I do everything that I'm about to do for you on video. All right? So let's get started. I'll try to make this as simple and pain-free as possible. And look, really, seriously, you want to do this. All right? This is a very valuable audience. All right. So here we are in the old Facebook, and here's how to create retargeting audiences. Not a lot to it. What you want to do, go to your hamburger-looking menu over here. Now, this is probably going to change immediately upon my recording this. But what we want to go to is audiences. All right? And there are a lot of ways that people can join your retargeting audience. So we're going to capitalize on all of them.
